Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to merge two event logs with different formats timestamps and attributes into a single unified log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key challenges are handling timestamp tolerances matching events across systems despite naming differences and enriching merged records with attributes from both logs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill use a 2 second tolerance for matching as suggested

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill map events by order ID and timing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order Received and OrderReceived are clearly the same event despite the 2 second difference

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order Validated and OrderValidation match closely too

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment Processed and PaymentCheck have a 5 second gap but given the notes mention a payment gateway delay this likely corresponds to the same event

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Item Shipped and Shipping align within tolerance

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Item Delivered has no match in Log B so it stays as a standalone A event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Quality Check is unique to Log B with no close match in Log A so it remains separate

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: For merged events Ill use the Log A timestamp as primary since its the received time but Ill include Log B timestamps in notes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Attribute merging will combine fields from both logs using placeholders for missing values

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final chronological order follows the primary timestamps with explanations for each match and non match decision
